model._comment: Schema source, named once. 'env' points into the environments registry by name (live OSSYS read, espace-safe) — the canonical environment the whole estate derives from. 'path' is the extracted-file fallback. Use 'ossys' instead of 'env' only for a standalone connection with no registry.

overrides._comment: Naming + structural directives, keyed to THIS sample's entities (Sales / Billing / ServiceCenter) — swap the refs for your own. Every section is optional; omit one to take the faithful default. A rename 'from' is EITHER logical { module, entity } OR physical { schema, table } (never both). 'migrationDependencies.path' names a separate operator-curated row inventory (see examples/migration-dependencies.sample.json) that feeds the MigrationData lane; those kinds are excluded from the bootstrap complement so the lanes stay disjoint.

emission._comment: WHAT the publish bundle emits, and HOW the data lanes write. ssdt = the schema; staticSeeds / migrationDependencies / bootstrap = the three data lanes (each defaults true; set one false to suppress that lane). The lanes are disjoint by construction: bootstrap covers the complement of (static ∪ migration), so no row loads twice. 'bootstrapAllData':true flips bootstrap to cover EVERY data-bearing kind — the full first-deploy snapshot (V1's AllEntitiesIncludingStatic) — and skips the static+migration lanes (default false). 'dataVerification':'validateBeforeApply' prepends a symmetric-EXCEPT drift guard (THROW 50000 on drift) ahead of every data MERGE; 'standard' (default) emits the MERGEs alone (byte-identical, CDC-silent on idempotent redeploy). 'deleteScope' adds a convergent 'WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE … DELETE' arm (a tenant/partition gate) to the static+migration MERGEs — omit it for the upsert-only default; bootstrap is additive-only and ignores it. 'sqlproj':true also drops an SDK-style Microsoft.Build.Sql project (ProjectionCatalog.sqlproj + Script.PostDeployment.sql, whose post-deploy :r-includes the static+migration lanes; bootstrap stays a separate post-publish step) so the publish bundle is a buildable SSDT project — 'dotnet build' → .dacpac (default false). 'renderDataElegant':true (default) formats the published Data/*.sql lanes in V1's readable shape — a SET NOCOUNT ON header, per-module banners, a '-- <Entity> (N rows)' comment per block, and the USING (VALUES …) list one row per line; 'false' passes the compact ScriptDom output through (diagnostic / bisect opt-out).
emission._signoff_comment: The emission-plane write-signoff greenlight (2026-07-09). A 'deleteScope' arm is the most destructive shape the publish bundle emits (a convergent 'WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE … DELETE'), so it is REFUSED (emission.deleteScope.ungreenlit) until 'delete-scope' is enumerated here — the emission-plane counterpart of a flow's 'signoff' (same WriteApproval vocabulary; a different config plane). Presence-gated: the arm's blast radius is the WHERE predicate above, not a table set, so 'tables' is accepted but not verified here. Omit both deleteScope and signoff for the upsert-only default.

signoff.acknowledgedImpact: the static/migration MERGEs delete every target row whose TenantId is not 42 — the tenant-partition converges to the source

emission._dataStaging_comment: HOW large data kinds write (static, migration, AND bootstrap lanes). A single inline 'MERGE … USING (VALUES …)' hits SQL Server error 8623 (the optimizer's plan-complexity wall) at ~25-30k rows, so above 'threshold' rows a kind stages its rows through a #temp table and runs ONE 'MERGE … USING #temp' (and, for cyclic kinds, a single set-based Phase-2 'UPDATE … FROM #fk') inside one atomic XACT_ABORT batch — no VALUES ceiling, all-or-nothing. 'mode':'auto' (default) stages above 'threshold' (default 1000); 'inline' NEVER stages (the locked-down / managed-OutSystems escape hatch — accepts the ~30k ceiling where even baseline #temp + BEGIN TRAN rights are unwanted); 'tempTable' ALWAYS stages. 'indexThreshold' (default 100000) is the row count above which a staged #temp also gets a CLUSTERED INDEX on its PK (built after the bulk insert, dropped with the #temp) so the MERGE merge-joins instead of hash-joining — measured ~33-37% faster at 100k/250k/500k; the default gates at the proven-win floor. The staged form needs only the baseline rights the identity-seed path already uses, so 'auto' is portable wherever the current seed path runs.

policy._comment: OPT-IN overlays. Omit this block for faithful vanilla emission. 'tightening.interventions' are TRACKED operator decisions (kinds: foreignKey / uniqueIndex / categoricalUniqueness); each carries a kind + id. 'transformGroups' BOTH run by default — a missing group is enabled — so list one with enabled:false only to suppress it.

profiler._comment: Source-data evidence. 'live' profiles the source DB (connection from PROJECTION_MSSQL_CONN_STR, never the config — D9) for the readiness data-dealbreaker checks and evidence-based tightening; 'fixture' (the default) carries none.

readiness._comment: The agreed shape defaults to model.env (cloud-dev) — name 'schema' here only to override it. 'confirm' lists the environments checked against that shape.

estate._comment: The repair band: past this many contradicting rows a fix becomes an interim 'leave it for now' config edit instead of a prepared repair. Default 100000. Set repairBandByEntity to tune it per table (a 200-row lookup and a billion-row fact table want different thresholds).

flows._supportingScope_comment: `supportingScope` (sibling of `tables`) declares WHY each SUPPORTING (non-payload) table is touched, in business terms; the go board verifies the intent against the relationship graph. References the payload points at: existing-reference (match the target's own rows by a business `key`, never copy), reference-seed (insert the referenced rows the target lacks), shared-anchor (re-point every reference to one target row named by `anchor`), static-lookup (match by `key` and hold identical — drift is a fault). Dependents that point at the payload: owned-child (`of` the parent; copied along, cascade-verified against the OutSystems delete rule), blocked-dependent (`of` the parent; deliberately not harvested — a confirmed invariant that a replace-wipe may still enforce). The terse `reconcile` strings are the shorthand for existing-reference / shared-anchor.
flows._signoff_comment: `signoff` (sibling of `tables`) is the first-class GREENLIGHT for a destructive write mode. A flow that will wipe rows (`replace` / `fresh`), drop rows (`drops`), flood the CDC ledger (`cdc`), write explicit keys (`identity-insert`), or converge-delete (`delete-scope`) must ENUMERATE that mode here — the operator declaring the impact is understood and approved. Default-on: a destructive Execute REFUSES and the go board goes RED (`transfer.writeSignoff.ungreenlit`) until the mode is greenlit. Each object carries the required `mode` plus an optional `tables` scope (empty = the whole flow's set for that mode — a declared scope is VERIFIED to cover the wipe, so a stale approval cannot rubber-stamp a wider blast radius), an optional `acknowledgedImpact` (echoed on the board), and optional `approvedBy` / `date` audit fields.

supportingScope.reason: scenes are part of the movie's definition
match the target's own users, never copy
countries must exist for the address relationship
audit logs are environment-specific; never harvested

signoff.acknowledgedImpact: the Movie payload and its owned scenes are wiped child-first and reloaded on cloud-uat; a target row absent from cloud-qa is removed

synthetic._comment: How `from: synthetic` generates data (THE_SYNTHETIC_DATA_DESIGN §11). Hybrid by cardinality: a column with ≤ preserveCardinalityMax distinct values keeps its real distribution; above that it is synthesized. 'preserve'/'synthesize' override per logical column NAME. --seed/--scale are the per-run overrides; the per-flow 'correction' carries the richer per-column PII→Faker intent (generate a first draft with `projection propose-correction`).
